## [00:00 - 03:03]

Okay, as we're winding down product launch formula here, we're getting to the end. I want to talk about the biggest thing, the most important thing, to getting to your first launch and then growing beyond that first launch and growing a business and getting to your launch life, you know, a life of great income, a great business, a great impact, and a great lifestyle. So the number one thing, and this might seem crazy and I know I wouldn't have believed it back when I first started in the mid-90s, that your mental game and your self-care are the most important things. Your growth is 100% about your inner game growth. The growth of your business is all about growing your inner game. Now, it doesn't mean you can just sit there and meditate, sit on the pillow and meditate and do that 24 hours a day and it's going to grow your business. But within PLF, you've gotten all the strategies and the tactics that you need to be able to market, to be able to put your product in front, to put a great product, a great offer in front of the market in a very, very powerful way so that they'll buy. But what I see holding people back is that they just don't get the mental game. They get scared. They worry about perfection. They just, they don't get what's going through their heads in the right place. So that's really what this video is about because one of the things is there's no perfect launch. When you get into a launch, you will learn so much. It's amazing, especially that first launch or the first three or four or five launches, you will learn so much. There is no perfect launch. People worry about getting it perfect. They worry about their product being perfect. They worry about their marketing being perfect. They worry about their launch being perfect. Their launch page, their opt-in page, their PLC. I've done, I don't know how many dozens of launches and every single one of them, I have made mistakes. I have had stuff that wasn't perfect. I've screwed stuff up. Every one of my products could have been better. But for some reason, and I tell you, I am a bit of a perfectionist, but I also started my business from absolute desperation, where I was desperate to make some money to try to support, help support the family in some way. And desperation is a way of pushing you past perfection, of pushing you to the situation where you give up on perfection. So I was lucky enough to be desperate. And I think out of that desperation and getting results from an imperfect launch and an imperfect imperfect offer, I just learned that at some point you actually have to just say, I'm done. You have to ship it. You have to put your launch out there, put your product out there. I can tell you, as I record this, I am about to start a launch tomorrow.

## [03:03 - 06:04]

And it's not perfect by any means. And I'm a little bit worried about it. But I also know I have to move it forward. This business is not about perfection. It's about taking baby steps. This is something you'll hear from me over and over, no matter where you're hearing from me, it's baby steps. It's just about moving forward. When a baby starts out, they never, when they go from crawling to walking, they never just start walking across the room. I don't know. If you watch a baby learning to walk, they'll stand up and they'll fall over. They'll stand up and fall over. They'll stand up and fall over. They'll stand up and take one step and then fall over. Then they'll take two steps and fall over. And they'll just keep on moving forward. Take those baby steps. You're not looking for the home run. Take those baby steps. Know that there will be failed launches. I hate to burst your bubble, but there will be failed launches. But the only way to truly fail is to not launch. Get that? The only way to really fail is if you don't launch. And the only way to really fail is if after that first failed launch, whether it's your first or your tenth, is to not launch again. I'm lucky. It took me probably 30 launches before I got to my first failed launch. And it wasn't a complete failure, but we underperformed in a big way. Took me 20 or 30 launches to get there, but it happened. Sooner or later, you're going to have one that doesn't go the way you want it to go. It's just going to happen. You just have to move forward and do the next launch. Fear is the thing that's going to hold you back. Fear, especially the fear, it shows up in the last 10%. It's like in American football. You know, in American football, we have this field here. And we have these end zones. And one team is trying to go that way, and the other team is trying to go this way. And you know what happens is it's really easy to go from here to here when you're moving that way. And it's easy to go to here. But then you get down here in this last 20%. It's what they call the red zone. And all of a sudden, it gets a lot harder. And when you get in the last 10%, it really gets hard. That's the way life is. That's the way business is. And that's the way launching is. You get this first 90% or even the first 80%. And you can move through that. But when you get to that last 10%, all of a sudden, the world seems to conspire to throw doubt and fear in front of you. And all of a sudden, you're wondering, is this going to work? Should I launch? What you have to know is that last 10% is hard for everyone. You just have to push through it. That is the time for willpower. That is the time to just move forward whether you're perfect or not.

## [06:04 - 09:08]

That last 10% is the hardest. The resistance here, as Steven Pressfield says in his genius book, The War of Art, that last 10% is where that resistance, that fear shows up. You just have to move through it. Know that everyone has it. Even after all these launches. Tomorrow I launch and today I'm scared. It's OK. It's OK to be scared. The only thing that matters is you take those baby steps and you move forward. One of the things you have to do during a launch, because a launch is a lot of work. And as you get down here, as you get towards the end, you're getting tired. You're running on fumes. It's been a long march. And so as you move through here, you have to work on the self-care. And this is something I'll tell you, the people at the highest level in this business, they're the ones that have learned the self-care. Because they've been through a lot of these and they've beaten themselves up. And they've worked themselves into the ground. And when you do that, you don't perform that well. And your mental game just flies out the window. So you have to be able to, you have to take care of yourself. You have to take care of yourself physically, mentally, spiritually. I think like one of the best, like for me, when I'm in a launch, I know when I get to a question that I go for a run. I go for a run in the woods. And what I say to me is the answer is in the woods. If I'm just like, I don't know what I'm going to actually say in this next email, that's the time for me to get away from my desk and get out and go for a run. So exercise, nutrition, hydration. For me, meditation, yoga, incredibly important. And as your level of success goes up, as you start to play a bigger and bigger and bigger game in your business. And maybe you start to grow your team. Maybe you've got a big following. As your level of success goes up, your need for self-care goes up. It's not like, wow, I've got this. I've been doing this stuff for five years now. I've got 20 launches behind me. I've built this great big business. I've got this. I can just, I can work 24 hours a day. Well, you can't. You need to sleep. You need to eat well. You need to drink water. You need to get exercise. For me, I need to get outside. I need to meditate. I need to take time and relax. I need to be with my family. All of these things, super, super, they're incredibly important. So don't forget about the self-care. One of my big rituals, one of the things I do, one of the habits I've got is I've got a morning ritual. So I get up in the morning and I have a ritual of self-care before I start working. And that includes meditation and yoga and hydration and nutrition and journaling and gratitude. And I don't do all of those every day, but I have, I basically said, this is my morning, these are my morning habits. This is my morning ritual. This is how I start the day to set myself up for a great day. So setting habits around self-care, super, super important.

## [09:08 - 12:15]

Continuous learning. Never just, that's what you signed up for. When you signed up to be an entrepreneur, when you signed up to be in business, you signed up for continuous learning. You just have to learn to embrace that. My friend Susan Garrett, who's in my Platinum Plus program, she's an incredible competitor, one of the most fierce competitors I know. And she likes to say, I never lose because I either am on the podium with a trophy, that's I win that way, or if I don't win and I'm not on the podium, then I've learned something and that means I've won either way. So the final thing I really want to leave you here is that, that I said this a few lessons ago, way leads on to way. There's no master plan. I had a huge pivot in my life where I went from figuring out how to have this stock market business to having my partner steal that business from me and not knowing what comes next, but knowing that I'd build up the skills to market, the skills to launch, the skills to build the business and having this huge pivot into what's now product launch formula. And now my business is, I think it's probably 40 or 50 times bigger than that old stock market business. And my life is way, way better. And I've had one pivot after another. I'm talking to you on video right now. Well, that first version of product launch formula, there was no video, a screen capture video. There's no full motion video. When I started my stock market business, there was no audio. No one for the first five, six, seven, eight years of my business, no one knew what I sounded like and no one knew what I looked like. There wasn't social media. There weren't photos online of people. There certainly wasn't video. I had no knowledge of shooting video, of creating video, of being on video. I just learned all that stuff. So things are going to change for you. You might be in a completely different market, a completely different business five years from now. But if you just focus on what you've learned in product launch formula and doing those launches and getting better and building your list and taking the long view and not chasing after a sale right now, but doing that long view, build your list. If you focus on progress instead of perfection, progress not perfection, if you focus on getting 90% right or 80% right and then moving on and learning, doing, learning, growing your skills, growing your mental game, then you're going to win in the long run. That's how I've done it. That's how I've gone from a stay at home dad to having a business that sold tens of millions of dollars of my products that I created to having clients that have launched a billion dollars worth of products. It's been a crazy journey, a crazy ride, but it's not just me. I've seen so many of my clients, of my launch club members, of my PLF owners, of my PLS

members, go on a similar path. The numbers might be different. The path might be slightly different, but this is all achievable for you. You've got it all here in PLF.
